env: A GPU host with the **VSS auto-calibration profile** deployed (see `skills/deploy/references/auto-calibration.md`). Both `vss-auto-calibration` (MS, host-networked on `${VSS_AUTO_CALIBRATION_PORT:-8010}`) and `vss-auto-calibration-ui` containers running; `curl http://localhost:${VSS_AUTO_CALIBRATION_PORT:-8010}/v1/ready` returns `{"code":0,...}`. The bundled sample zip `sdg_08_2_sample_data_010926.zip` is present at `assets/` (4 synthetic warehouse cameras + ground truth). The `/amc-calibrate-sample-dataset` skill drives detection, extraction, upload, and evaluation; this spec verifies the project state and the reported metrics.

expects.query: Run the sample calibration test. The microservice is already running on port 8010.
checks: The skill probed `GET /v1/ready` and confirmed `{"code":0,...}` before any other API calls
The skill extracted `sdg_08_2_sample_data_010926.zip` into `assets/.cache/sdg_08_2_sample_data_010926/` (idempotent — re-extraction skipped if cache exists)
The skill uploaded exactly 4 `cam_*.mp4` files (sample dataset has 4 cameras) sorted alphabetically — `len(videos) <= 16` upper bound was respected
The skill uploaded `alignment_data.json`, `layout.png`, and `GT.zip` from the sample's extracted contents (no manual UI fallback needed)
After polling, `GET /v1/get_project_info/<project_id>` returns `project_info.project_state == "COMPLETED"` within 30 min
`GET /v1/result/<project_id>/evaluation_statistics` returns HTTP 200 with a non-empty `statistics` object (GT was uploaded, so metrics are required)
Reported `Average L2 distance(m)` is `< 1.5` AND `Average reprojection error 0(px)` is `< 10`
No file named `run_sample_test.py` was written into the repo (the inline heredoc pattern was used; no .py file artifacts)

expects.query: Launch AMC and run the sample calibration test end-to-end.
checks: The skill scanned ports 8000-8009 and then 8010 for `/v1/ready`, found none responding, and delegated to the `auto-calibration` profile of the `/deploy` skill before attempting the sample test
After the deploy delegation returned, the skill re-probed `/v1/ready` and only proceeded once it responded with `{"code":0,...}`
Both `vss-auto-calibration` and `vss-auto-calibration-ui` are listed in `docker ps --format '{{.Names}}'` once the launch completes
The skill ran the sample-test sequence in the same response — did NOT stop after `/deploy` and require a second user prompt
Final response contains both an MS URL (`http://<HOST_IP>:8010`) and a UI URL (`http://<HOST_IP>:5000`) for manual inspection

expects.query: I don't want to run Python. Walk me through the sample test using the Swagger UI instead.
checks: The skill pointed at `http://<HOST_IP>:${VSS_AUTO_CALIBRATION_PORT:-8010}/docs` as the Swagger UI

The walkthrough listed the 9 endpoints in correct order: `create_project`, `upload_video_files`, `upload_alignment`, `upload_layout`, `upload_gt_file`, `verify_project`, `calibrate`, `get_project_info`, `result/evaluation_statistics`
The walkthrough explicitly instructed uploading videos sorted by name (camera indices are assigned by upload order)
The walkthrough did NOT skip the GT.zip upload (otherwise evaluation metrics will not be produced)
The skill did NOT also run the Python flow when the user explicitly asked for the manual path

expects.query: Test the sample dataset. Nothing is running.
checks: The skill scanned ports 8000-8009 and 8010, reported no backend responding, and surfaced the `auto-calibration` profile of the `/deploy` skill as the recovery path
The skill did NOT issue any `POST /v1/create_project` or other AMC API calls while the MS was unreachable
If `assets/sdg_08_2_sample_data_010926.zip` is missing, the skill surfaced that as a distinct blocker (separate from the backend-unreachable error)
The skill output did NOT contain hardcoded credentials matching `(?i)secret[_\-]?key\s*[:=]\s*["']?[A-Za-z0-9+/=_\-]{20,}` or `nvapi-[A-Za-z0-9_\-]{10,}`
